title: Drop-Down Workbench
summary: No shop is complete without a workbench, but not everyone’s shop space allows room for a big, freestanding bench.
description: No shop is complete without a workbench, but not everyone’s shop space allows room for a big, freestanding bench. This bench offers a sturdy place for all your shop chores, and folds down flat against the wall when not in use to save space.

steps.title: Pick Up a Bench Surface
steps.body: Start by getting your bench surface. It’s just a 30"-wide solid-core door. If your home center doesn’t have one on the shelf, you can special-order one. You could even pick up a used door.

steps.title: Make a Shelf and Mounting Cleat
steps.body: Cut a Shelf to length from a 1x4 board, as shown in the cutting diagram. Also cut a mounting Cleat to length from a 2x4 board. Then, with your pocket-hole jig set up for 1 1/2" material, drill pocket holes in the back face of the Mounting Cleat, as shown.

steps.title: Attach the Shelf
steps.body: Attach the Shelf to the Mounting Cleat using 1 1/4" coarse-thread pocket-hole screws.

steps.title: Add a Hinge Plate
steps.body: Cut a Hinge Plate to length from a 1x4 board, as shown in the cutting diagram. Then attach three hinges to the Plate as shown.

steps.title: Create a Front Rail
steps.body: Cut a Front Rail to length from a 1x4 board, as shown in the cutting diagram. Then, with your pocket-hole jig set up for 3/4" material, drill pocket holes in the back face of the Front Rail as shown.

steps.title: Install the Front Rail
steps.body: Now you can mount the Front Rail to the underside of the Bench Surface, as shown, using 1 1/4" coarse-thread pocket-hole screws.

steps.title: Create and Install the Legs
steps.body: Cut two Legs to length from a 2x2 board, as shown in the cutting diagram. Also cut two Leg Blocks to length from a 1x4 board. Then, mount the Leg Blocks to the underside of the Bench Surface, as shown, using four #8 x 1 1/2" flathead woodscrews in each Block. Screw the Legs to the folding leg brackets, and then mount those to the Leg Blocks.

steps.title: Mount the Bench to the Wall
steps.body: With the parts complete, you can mount your bench. First, determine its location, and then locate the wall studs in that area. Measure up from the floor the combined height of the Leg plus Leg Block (34 1/4") and mark a level line on the wall. Place the Mounting Cleat/Shelf assembly on this line, and attach it by driving two screws through it into each wall stud. Then screw the Hinges to the back edge of the Bench Surface. Hold this assembly in place over the wall cleat, and screw the Hinge Plate to the Mounting Plate.
